deployments.title: List deployments

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: sha
parameters.description: The SHA recorded at creation time.

parameters.name: ref
parameters.description: The name of the ref. This can be a branch, tag, or SHA.

parameters.name: task
parameters.description: The name of the task for the deployment (e.g., deploy or deploy:migrations).

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: The name of the environment that was deployed to (e.g., staging or production).

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

deployments.descriptionHTML: Simple filtering of deployments is available via query parameters:

items.title: Deployment
items.description: A request for a specific ref(branch,sha,tag) to be deployed

id.description: Unique identifier of the deployment

ref.description: The ref to deploy. This can be a branch, tag, or sha.

task.description: Parameter to specify a task to execute

environment.description: Name for the target deployment environment.

anyOf.title: Simple User
anyOf.description: A GitHub user.

transient_environment.description: Specifies if the given environment is will no longer exist at some point in the future. Default: false.

production_environment.description: Specifies if the given environment is one that end-users directly interact with. Default: false.

anyOf.title: GitHub app
anyOf.description: GitHub apps are a new way to extend GitHub. They can be installed directly on organizations and user accounts and granted access to specific repositories. They come with granular permissions and built-in webhooks. GitHub apps are first class actors within GitHub.

id.description: Unique identifier of the GitHub app

slug.description: The slug name of the GitHub app

description.description: A short description of the enterprise.

id.description: Unique identifier of the enterprise

name.description: The name of the enterprise.

slug.description: The slug url identifier for the enterprise.

name.description: The name of the GitHub app

permissions.description: The set of permissions for the GitHub app

events.description: The list of events for the GitHub app. Note that the `installation_target`, `security_advisory`, and `meta` events are not included because they are global events and not specific to an installation.

installations_count.description: The number of installations associated with the GitHub app. Only returned when the integration is requesting details about itself.

deployments.title: Create a deployment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: ref
bodyParameters.description: The ref to deploy. This can be a branch, tag, or SHA.

bodyParameters.name: task
bodyParameters.description: Specifies a task to execute (e.g., deploy or deploy:migrations).

bodyParameters.name: auto_merge
bodyParameters.description: Attempts to automatically merge the default branch into the requested ref, if it's behind the default branch.

bodyParameters.name: required_contexts
bodyParameters.description: The status contexts to verify against commit status checks. If you omit this parameter, GitHub verifies all unique contexts before creating a deployment. To bypass checking entirely, pass an empty array. Defaults to all unique contexts.

bodyParameters.name: payload
bodyParameters.description: JSON payload with extra information about the deployment.

bodyParameters.name: environment
bodyParameters.description: Name for the target deployment environment (e.g., production, staging, qa).

bodyParameters.name: description
bodyParameters.description: Short description of the deployment.

bodyParameters.name: transient_environment
bodyParameters.description: Specifies if the given environment is specific to the deployment and will no longer exist at some point in the future. Default: false

bodyParameters.name: production_environment
bodyParameters.description: Specifies if the given environment is one that end-users directly interact with. Default: true when environment is production and false otherwise.

deployments.descriptionHTML:
Deployments offer a few configurable parameters with certain defaults.

The ref parameter can be any named branch, tag, or SHA. At GitHub Enterprise Server we often deploy branches and verify them before we merge a pull request.

The environment parameter allows deployments to be issued to different runtime environments. Teams often have multiple environments for verifying their applications, such as production, staging, and qa. This parameter makes it easier to track which environments have requested deployments. The default environment is production.

The auto_merge parameter is used to ensure that the requested ref is not behind the repository's default branch. If the ref is behind the default branch for the repository, we will attempt to merge it for you. If the merge succeeds, the API will return a successful merge commit. If merge conflicts prevent the merge from succeeding, the API will return a failure response.

By default, commit statuses for every submitted context must be in a success state. The required_contexts parameter allows you to specify a subset of contexts that must be success, or to specify contexts that have not yet been submitted. You are not required to use commit statuses to deploy. If you do not require any contexts or create any commit statuses, the deployment will always succeed.

The payload parameter is available for any extra information that a deployment system might need. It is a JSON text field that will be passed on when a deployment event is dispatched.

The task parameter is used by the deployment system to allow different execution paths. In the web world this might be deploy:migrations to run schema changes on the system. In the compiled world this could be a flag to compile an application with debugging enabled.

Merged branch response:

You will see this response when GitHub automatically merges the base branch into the topic branch instead of creating a deployment. This auto-merge happens when:

  • Auto-merge option is enabled in the repository
  • Topic branch does not include the latest changes on the base branch, which is master in the response example
  • There are no merge conflicts

If there are no new commits in the base branch, a new request to create a deployment should give a successful response.

Merge conflict response:

This error happens when the auto_merge option is enabled and when the default branch (in this case master), can't be merged into the branch that's being deployed (in this case topic-branch), due to merge conflicts.

Failed commit status checks:

This error happens when the required_contexts parameter indicates that one or more contexts need to have a success status for the commit to be deployed, but one or more of the required contexts do not have a state of success.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repo_deployment scope to use this endpoint.

bodyParameters.description: Deploy request from hubot

deployments.title: Delete a deployment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: deployment_id
parameters.description: deployment_id parameter

deployments.descriptionHTML:
If the repository only has one deployment, you can delete the deployment regardless of its status. If the repository has more than one deployment, you can only delete inactive deployments. This ensures that repositories with multiple deployments will always have an active deployment.

To set a deployment as inactive, you must:

  • Create a new deployment that is active so that the system has a record of the current state, then delete the previously active deployment.
  • Mark the active deployment as inactive by adding any non-successful deployment status.

For more information, see "Create a deployment" and "Create a deployment status."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo or repo_deployment scope to use this endpoint.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

branch-policies.title: List deployment branch policies

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: environment_name
parameters.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes in the name must be replaced with %2F.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

branch-policies.descriptionHTML:
Lists the deployment branch policies for an environment.

Anyone with read access to the repository can use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint with a private repository.

total_count.description: The number of deployment branch policies for the environment.

items.title: Deployment branch policy
items.description: Details of a deployment branch or tag policy.

id.description: The unique identifier of the branch or tag policy.

name.description: The name pattern that branches or tags must match in order to deploy to the environment.

type.description: Whether this rule targets a branch or tag.

branch-policies.title: Create a deployment branch policy

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: environment_name
parameters.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes in the name must be replaced with %2F.

bodyParameters.name: name
bodyParameters.description:
The name pattern that branches or tags must match in order to deploy to the environment.

Wildcard characters will not match /. For example, to match branches that begin with release/ and contain an additional single slash, use release/*/*. For more information about pattern matching syntax, see the Ruby File.fnmatch documentation.

bodyParameters.name: type
bodyParameters.description: Whether this rule targets a branch or tag

branch-policies.descriptionHTML:
Creates a deployment branch or tag policy for an environment.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

environments.title: List environments

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number of the results to fetch. For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

environments.descriptionHTML:
Lists the environments for a repository.

Anyone with read access to the repository can use this endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint with a private repository.

total_count.description: The number of environments in this repository

id.description: The id of the environment.

name.description: The name of the environment.

created_at.description: The time that the environment was created, in ISO 8601 format.

updated_at.description: The time that the environment was last updated, in ISO 8601 format.

protection_rules.description: Built-in deployment protection rules for the environment.

wait_timer.description: The amount of time to delay a job after the job is initially triggered. The time (in minutes) must be an integer between 0 and 43,200 (30 days).

prevent_self_review.description: Whether deployments to this environment can be approved by the user who created the deployment.

reviewers.description: The people or teams that may approve jobs that reference the environment. You can list up to six users or teams as reviewers. The reviewers must have at least read access to the repository. Only one of the required reviewers needs to approve the job for it to proceed.

anyOf.title: Simple User
anyOf.description: A GitHub user.

anyOf.title: Team
anyOf.description: Groups of organization members that gives permissions on specified repositories.

type.description: The ownership type of the team

organization_id.description: Unique identifier of the organization to which this team belongs

enterprise_id.description: Unique identifier of the enterprise to which this team belongs

anyOf.title: Team Simple
anyOf.description: Groups of organization members that gives permissions on specified repositories.

id.description: Unique identifier of the team

name.description: Name of the team

permission.description: Permission that the team will have for its repositories

privacy.description: The level of privacy this team should have

notification_setting.description: The notification setting the team has set

ldap_dn.description: Distinguished Name (DN) that team maps to within LDAP environment

type.description: The ownership type of the team

organization_id.description: Unique identifier of the organization to which this team belongs

enterprise_id.description: Unique identifier of the enterprise to which this team belongs

deployment_branch_policy.description: The type of deployment branch policy for this environment. To allow all branches to deploy, set to `null`.

protected_branches.description: Whether only branches with branch protection rules can deploy to this environment. If `protected_branches` is `true`, `custom_branch_policies` must be `false`; if `protected_branches` is `false`, `custom_branch_policies` must be `true`.

custom_branch_policies.description: Whether only branches that match the specified name patterns can deploy to this environment.  If `custom_branch_policies` is `true`, `protected_branches` must be `false`; if `custom_branch_policies` is `false`, `protected_branches` must be `true`.

Note

To get information about name patterns that branches must match in order to deploy to this environment, see "Get a deployment branch policy."

environments.title: Create or update an environment

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: environment_name
parameters.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes in the name must be replaced with %2F.

bodyParameters.name: wait_timer
bodyParameters.description: The amount of time to delay a job after the job is initially triggered. The time (in minutes) must be an integer between 0 and 43,200 (30 days).

bodyParameters.name: prevent_self_review
bodyParameters.description: Whether or not a user who created the job is prevented from approving their own job.

bodyParameters.name: reviewers
bodyParameters.description: The people or teams that may review jobs that reference the environment. You can list up to six users or teams as reviewers. The reviewers must have at least read access to the repository. Only one of the required reviewers needs to approve the job for it to proceed.

childParamsGroups.name: id
childParamsGroups.description: The id of the user or team who can review the deployment

bodyParameters.name: deployment_branch_policy
bodyParameters.description: The type of deployment branch policy for this environment. To allow all branches to deploy, set to null.

childParamsGroups.name: protected_branches
childParamsGroups.description: Whether only branches with branch protection rules can deploy to this environment. If protected_branches is true, custom_branch_policies must be false; if protected_branches is false, custom_branch_policies must be true.

childParamsGroups.name: custom_branch_policies
childParamsGroups.description: Whether only branches that match the specified name patterns can deploy to this environment. If custom_branch_policies is true, protected_branches must be false; if custom_branch_policies is false, protected_branches must be true.

environments.descriptionHTML:
Create or update an environment with protection rules, such as required reviewers. For more information about environment protection rules, see "Environments."

Note

To create or update name patterns that branches must match in order to deploy to this environment, see "Deployment branch policies."

Note

To create or update secrets for an environment, see "GitHub Actions secrets."

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

protection-rules.title: Get all deployment protection rules for an environment

parameters.name: environment_name
parameters.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes in the name must be replaced with %2F.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

protection-rules.descriptionHTML:
Gets all custom deployment protection rules that are enabled for an environment. Anyone with read access to the repository can use this endpoint. For more information about environments, see "Using environments for deployment."

For more information about the app that is providing this custom deployment rule, see the documentation for the GET /apps/{app_slug} endpoint.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint with a private repository.

total_count.description: The number of enabled custom deployment protection rules for this environment

node_id.description: The node ID for the deployment protection rule.

protection-rules.title: Create a custom deployment protection rule on an environment

parameters.name: environment_name
parameters.description: The name of the environment. The name must be URL encoded. For example, any slashes in the name must be replaced with %2F.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: integration_id
bodyParameters.description: The ID of the custom app that will be enabled on the environment.

protection-rules.descriptionHTML:
Enable a custom deployment protection rule for an environment.

The authenticated user must have admin or owner permissions to the repository to use this endpoint.

For more information about the app that is providing this custom deployment rule, see the documentation for the GET /apps/{app_slug} endpoint, as well as the guide to creating custom deployment protection rules.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint.

protection-rules.descriptionHTML:
Gets all custom deployment protection rule integrations that are available for an environment.

The authenticated user must have admin or owner permissions to the repository to use this endpoint.

For more information about environments, see "Using environments for deployment."

For more information about the app that is providing this custom deployment rule, see "GET an app".

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo scope to use this endpoint with a private repository.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: deployment_id
parameters.description: deployment_id parameter

parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100). For more information, see "Using pagination in the REST API."

statuses.descriptionHTML: Users with pull access can view deployment statuses for a deployment:

anyOf.title: Simple User
anyOf.description: A GitHub user.

description.description: A short description of the status.

environment.description: The environment of the deployment that the status is for.

target_url.description: Closing down notice: the URL to associate with this status.

environment_url.description: The URL for accessing your environment.

log_url.description: The URL to associate with this status.

anyOf.title: GitHub app
anyOf.description: GitHub apps are a new way to extend GitHub. They can be installed directly on organizations and user accounts and granted access to specific repositories. They come with granular permissions and built-in webhooks. GitHub apps are first class actors within GitHub.

id.description: Unique identifier of the GitHub app

slug.description: The slug name of the GitHub app

description.description: A short description of the enterprise.

id.description: Unique identifier of the enterprise

name.description: The name of the enterprise.

slug.description: The slug url identifier for the enterprise.

name.description: The name of the GitHub app

permissions.description: The set of permissions for the GitHub app

events.description: The list of events for the GitHub app. Note that the `installation_target`, `security_advisory`, and `meta` events are not included because they are global events and not specific to an installation.

installations_count.description: The number of installations associated with the GitHub app. Only returned when the integration is requesting details about itself.

statuses.title: Create a deployment status

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: deployment_id
parameters.description: deployment_id parameter

bodyParameters.name: state
bodyParameters.description: The state of the status. When you set a transient deployment to inactive, the deployment will be shown as destroyed in GitHub.

bodyParameters.name: target_url
bodyParameters.description:
The target URL to associate with this status. This URL should contain output to keep the user updated while the task is running or serve as historical information for what happened in the deployment.

Note

It's recommended to use the log_url parameter, which replaces target_url.

bodyParameters.name: log_url
bodyParameters.description: The full URL of the deployment's output. This parameter replaces target_url. We will continue to accept target_url to support legacy uses, but we recommend replacing target_url with log_url. Setting log_url will automatically set target_url to the same value. Default: ""

bodyParameters.name: description
bodyParameters.description: A short description of the status. The maximum description length is 140 characters.

bodyParameters.name: environment
bodyParameters.description: Name for the target deployment environment, which can be changed when setting a deploy status. For example, production, staging, or qa. If not defined, the environment of the previous status on the deployment will be used, if it exists. Otherwise, the environment of the deployment will be used.

bodyParameters.name: environment_url
bodyParameters.description: Sets the URL for accessing your environment. Default: ""

bodyParameters.name: auto_inactive
bodyParameters.description: Adds a new inactive status to all prior non-transient, non-production environment deployments with the same repository and environment name as the created status's deployment. An inactive status is only added to deployments that had a success state. Default: true

statuses.descriptionHTML:
Users with push access can create deployment statuses for a given deployment.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the repo_deployment scope to use this endpoint.

bodyParameters.description: Deployment finished successfully.

anyOf.description: A GitHub user.

description.description: A short description of the status.

environment.description: The environment of the deployment that the status is for.

target_url.description: Closing down notice: the URL to associate with this status.

environment_url.description: The URL for accessing your environment.

log_url.description: The URL to associate with this status.

permissions.description: The set of permissions for the GitHub app

installations_count.description: The number of installations associated with the GitHub app. Only returned when the integration is requesting details about itself.
